en: What do you need me to do? Whatever you have to, to get Ava out, because the camera's going to try and keep her in. You really think he's going to try and deny her bail?
ko: 뭘 해야 돼? 에이바를 빼내. 카메라가 잡으려 할 테니까. 보석 거부할 거라 생각해?

en: No, Harvey, one thing- Hey, look, if you have a problem defending someone who's accused of murder, I don't want to hear it. Look, you won't, because I'm willing to do whatever it takes to win, whether she's innocent or not.
ko: 아니, 하비, 하나만- 살인 혐의 변호에 문제가 있으면 듣기 싫어. 유무죄 상관없이 이기기 위해 뭐든 할 거야.

en: Then come with me. Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. I'm going to see Ava with you? Yep. Yeah, baby.
ko: 같이 가. 잠깐, 에이바한테 같이 가? 응. 좋지.

en: Butch and Sundance are back. Ooh, no go. What do you mean, no go? I mean, you're not Butch or Sundance.
ko: 부치와 선댄스가 돌아왔어. 안 돼. 무슨 소리야? 넌 부치도 선댄스도 아니야.

en: Why not? Because Butch was the leader, and Sundance was the gunslinger, and I'm both. Okay, but Sundance couldn't swim, and Butch never shot anyone, so that makes you a loser.
ko: 왜? 부치가 리더고 선댄스가 총잡이인데 난 둘 다거든. 근데 선댄스는 수영 못 하고 부치는 총 안 쐈잖아, 그럼 넌 루저야.

en: Better than a fraud. I think it's time for some new material. You write Midnight Train to Georgia, you don't just sing it once.
ko: 사기꾼보단 낫지. 새 레퍼토리가 필요해. 조지아행 야간열차를 만들면 한 번만 부르진 않잖아.

en: And speaking of Butch Cassidy, let's go start a knife fight. Did you know about these murders?
ko: 부치 캐시디 얘기가 나왔으니 칼싸움이나 하러 가자. 이 살인 사건 알고 있었어?

en: I knew there was a demonstration.      It turned ugly, and that people died. Did you know those people were the leaders of the demonstration?
ko: 시위가 있었고 험악해져서 사람들이 죽었다는 건 알았어. 그 사람들이 시위 지도자인 건 알았어?

en: No. Did you know the military was responsible? All I know about these deaths is that they happened.
ko: 아니. 군대가 책임진 건? 내가 아는 건 그 죽음이 일어났다는 것뿐이야.

en: On your land? We own thousands of acres. It's a volatile country. People kill each other on a regular basis.
ko: 당신 땅에서? 수천 에이커를 소유하고 있어. 불안정한 나라야. 사람들이 일상적으로 서로 죽여.

en: So you just chalk it up to coincidence that the six leaders of this demonstration, against your company's policies, on your company's private property, were murdered?
ko: 당신 회사 정책에 반대하는 시위의 여섯 지도자가 당신 회사 사유지에서 살해된 게 우연이라고?

en: It was reported as a combat incident, not a murder. You just said all you know about these deaths is that they happened.
ko: 전투 사건으로 보고됐어, 살인이 아니라. 아까 아는 거라곤 죽음이 일어났다는 것뿐이라며.

en: Now you know it was a combat incident. Which one is it? Harvey, what the hell is this? And who the hell does he think he is?
ko: 이젠 전투 사건이라고? 어느 쪽이야? 하비, 이게 뭐야? 이 사람은 누구야?

en: Mike's just doing his job. Well, Mike, every death in that country is reported as a combat incident.
ko: 마이크는 자기 일을 하는 거야. 마이크, 그 나라에서 모든 사망은 전투 사건으로 보고돼.

en: And for your information, there were thousands of people opposed to our pipeline. That's all we need right now.
ko: 참고로, 우리 파이프라인에 반대하는 사람이 수천 명이었어. 지금은 이 정도면 돼.

en: Would you please excuse us? How exactly did you manage to get your head so far up your own ass?
ko: 잠시 자리 좀 비켜줘. 대체 어떻게 그렇게 멍청한 짓을 한 거야?

en: You told me to take this deal. I did. And now here I am. I didn't know about these murders.
ko: 이 건을 맡으라고 해서 맡았어. 그런데 여기 있잖아. 이 살인 사건 몰랐어.

en: What you don't seem to believe is neither did I. We were doing our job. Cameron Dennis will come at you with worse.
ko: 나도 몰랐다는 걸 안 믿는 거 같은데. 우리는 일을 하고 있었어. 캐머런 데니스가 더 심하게 나올 거야.

en: Do you think I did this? That's irrelevant. I just want to make sure I know all the evidence against you.
ko: 내가 했다고 생각해? 그건 상관없어. 불리한 증거를 다 알고 싶을 뿐이야.

en: I have no idea what the evidence is because I had nothing to do with it. And you say it's irrelevant, but it's not irrelevant to me.
ko: 증거가 뭔지 몰라, 난 관계없으니까. 상관없다고 하지만 내겐 상관있어.

en: Do you think I did this? Do you want a lawyer who thinks you're innocent and loses? Or a lawyer who doesn't want to know and wins?
ko: 내가 했다고 생각해? 당신이 무죄라고 믿고 지는 변호사를 원해? 아니면 알고 싶지 않고 이기는 변호사?

en: You know the answer to that. Then don't ask me that question again. Because after I win and you're free, what I think won't matter.
ko: 답은 알잖아. 그럼 다시 묻지 마. 이기고 풀려나면 내 생각은 상관없을 테니.

en: Here it is. Fully executed settlement. Signed, sealed, delivered.
ko: 여기 있어. 완전히 체결된 합의서. 서명 완료.

en: Who'd you get to represent the other side? Harold Gunderson. Lewis' whipping boy? Yeah, he works for Alison Holt now.
ko: 상대방은 누가 맡았어? 해롤드 건더슨. 루이스의 하수인? 앨리슨 홀트 밑에서 일해.

en: Which is actually kind of perfect because everyone knows she would never do anything to help us. But I got Harold the job, so... He owes you one.
ko: 사실 완벽해. 앨리슨은 절대 우리를 도울 사람이 아니니까. 근데 내가 해롤드한테 자리를 줬으니... 빚을 졌지.

en: Not anymore. What'd you say? What?
ko: 이제 아니야. 뭐라고 했어? 뭘?

en: When Ava asked if I thought she was guilty. I told her that I had no idea what you thought and it was none of her business what I thought.
ko: 에이바가 내 생각을 물었을 때. 네 생각은 모르겠고 내 생각은 상관없다고 했어.

en: Good answer. What about you?
ko: 좋은 대답이야. 넌?

en: You want to know what I think? I know what you think. And I think it too. She did it.
ko: 내 생각을 알고 싶어? 네 생각 알아. 나도 그렇게 생각해. 그녀가 했어.

en: What's going on with Ava Hesington? I convinced her not to give an interview. How'd she get it in her head it'd be a good idea in the first place.
ko: 에이바 헤싱턴은 어떻게 돼? 인터뷰 안 하게 설득했어. 처음에 왜 좋은 생각이라고 했대?

en: Lewis. Is he aware she's up for murder? Is he aware that most people don't line their offices with pictures of themselves?
ko: 루이스야. 살인 혐의인 거 알아? 사무실에 자기 사진을 도배하는 걸 알아?

en: Evidently not. You know he's still got one of his dead cat. Harvey, leave the cat alone. Stupid cat.
ko: 분명 모르지. 아직 죽은 고양이 사진도 있어. 하비, 고양이는 놔둬. 멍청한 고양이.

en: How dare you sabotage my case? I didn't sabotage shit. You set that thing up behind my back and you didn't tell me about it until you thought it was too late.
ko: 감히 내 사건을 방해해? 방해 안 했어. 내 등 뒤에서 꾸미고 늦었다고 생각할 때까지 안 알려줬잖아.

en: Yeah, because I knew the minute things heated up on the takeover attempt you'd want to take my case back. Take your case back? I don't know how familiar you are with the game of law, but murder trumps takeover.
ko: 인수 시도가 달아오르면 내 사건을 되찾으려 할 줄 알았으니까. 되찾다고? 법의 게임에서 살인이 인수보다 우선이야.

en: Oh, you condescending piece of shit. Harvey's right. The woman can't make public statements while she's on trial for murder.
ko: 이 건방진 놈. 하비 말이 맞아. 살인 재판 중에 공개 발언은 안 돼.

en: Trump. Well, if she's going to keep her mouth shut, then she's going to have to step down or we're going to lose this vote. Asking her to step down would be asking her to cut off her arm.
ko: 트럼프. 입을 다물면 물러나야 해, 안 그러면 투표에서 져. 물러나라는 건 팔을 자르라는 거야.

en: And if we don't cut off her arm, Harvey, we're going to lose the patient. She's my client. I'm not going to let that happen. Ava Hesington is not my client.
ko: 팔을 안 자르면 환자를 잃어. 내 의뢰인이야. 그렇게 못 하게 해. 에이바 헤싱턴은 내 의뢰인이 아니야.

en: Hesington Oil is. And they generate $14 million a year in billables. Have you not been listening?
ko: 헤싱턴 오일이야. 연간 1400만 달러 청구 수수료를 벌어. 안 들었어?

en: Ava Hesington is Hesington Oil. Not for long. Excuse me? Lewis is right. If she steps down now, we can still win the vote and keep them as a client.
ko: 에이바 헤싱턴이 곧 헤싱턴 오일이야. 오래 못 가. 뭐라고? 루이스 말이 맞아. 지금 물러나면 투표에서 이기고 고객을 유지할 수 있어.

en: Well, who's going to convince her to do that? We don't have to convince her. We can just go to the board. We don't even have to tell her. Like hell you will.
ko: 누가 설득하지? 설득할 필요 없어. 이사회에 직접 가면 돼. 알릴 필요도 없어. 절대 안 돼.

en: And like hell I won't. Harvey, this needs to happen. No. I won't do it. This is my call.
ko: 절대 안 된다고. 하비, 이건 해야 해. 아니. 안 해. 내 결정이야.

en: I'm afraid it isn't. Excuse me? You and I both know who assigned me to this case.
ko: 유감이지만 아니야. 뭐라고? 이 사건을 맡긴 사람이 누군지 우리 둘 다 알잖아.

en: And it wasn't you. It was the man you chose to merge with. The same man who has a 51% share of our law firm.
ko: 너가 아니야. 네가 합병하기로 한 사람이야. 우리 로펌의 51%를 가진 사람.

en: So, unless you're going to undo that, you're going to live with this. Have you seen this?
ko: 그걸 되돌리지 않을 거면 이걸 받아들여야 해. 이거 봤어?

en: It's an article in today's Wall Street Journal painting me as the pitcher of corporate greed. As I told you before, if you speak out, they're going to just... I have the chance to save my company.
ko: 오늘 월스트리트 저널에 나를 기업 탐욕의 상징으로 그린 기사야. 전에 말했잖아, 발언하면... 회사를 살릴 기회가 있어.

en: Lewis Litt is doing that right now. That is not the point. That is the point. We can still save your company and you won't have to hurt yourself at your murder trial.
ko: 루이스 리트가 지금 하고 있어. 그게 중요한 게 아니야. 그게 중요해. 회사를 살리면서 살인 재판에서 불리하지 않을 수 있어.

en: No, the point is, I want to speak at my murder trial. That's not wise. And how many murder trials have you conducted in your many years?
ko: 아니, 중요한 건 내가 살인 재판에서 발언하고 싶다는 거야. 현명하지 않아. 오랜 경력 동안 살인 재판을 몇 번이나 했어?

en: Silence is damning. Look at this article. I'm already guilty. If you take the stand, Cameron Dennis is going to rip you to shreds.
ko: 침묵은 유죄나 다름없어. 이 기사 봐. 이미 유죄야. 증인석에 서면 캐머런 데니스가 갈기갈기 찢을 거야.

en: This is not up for discussion. I will have my say. Then I'm going to show you what you're in for. Well, you wouldn't be doing your job if you didn't.
ko: 논의 대상이 아니야. 내가 발언할 거야. 그럼 어떤 상황인지 보여줄게. 변호사로서 당연히 해야지.

en: Is that really necessary? It'll be in the deposition. I don't care about the deposition. Harvey.
ko: 꼭 필요해? 증언 녹취에 나올 거야. 녹취는 상관없어. 하비.

en: You want to know how you come across, you have to see how you come across. Now answer my question. Did you bribe a foreign government?
ko: 어떻게 보이는지 알려면 직접 봐야 해. 내 질문에 답해. 외국 정부에 뇌물을 줬어?

en: Why are you hesitating, Ms. Hesington? Something to hide? Look, I think what Harvey's trying to say is... You hesitate, they won't trust you.
ko: 왜 망설여요, 헤싱턴 씨? 숨길 게 있어? 저기, 하비가 말하려는 건... 망설이면 신뢰를 잃어.

en: Your bribery isn't in question. Cameron is going to enter that plea into evidence. You have to own it and move on. And I suggest you do it more believably.
ko: 뇌물은 문제가 아니야. 캐머런이 증거로 제출할 거야. 인정하고 넘어가야 해. 더 설득력 있게 해.

en: Because when you're up there, staring down the barrel of 12 executioners, there's no changing your mind.
ko: 증인석에서 12명의 처형자를 마주하면 마음을 바꿀 수 없으니까.

en: You think I don't know you're trying to rattle me? Rattle you? I'm just giving you the facts. Cameron's going to rattle you.
ko: 겁주려는 거 아는데? 겁을 준다고? 사실을 말하는 거야. 캐머런이 겁을 줄 거야.

en: And it's going to work. Because you and I both know you did this. How dare you? How dare I what?
ko: 그리고 효과가 있을 거야. 당신이 했다는 걸 우리 둘 다 아니까. 어떻게 감히? 감히 뭐?

en: This is your idea. Then ask me a goddamn question. You bribed that government. What's to stop anyone with a pulse from believing you knew that money was payment for killing those people?
ko: 당신이 원한 거야. 그럼 질문 해봐. 정부에 뇌물을 줬잖아. 그 돈이 살인 대가가 아니라고 누가 믿겠어?

en: Because life is more important to me than money. Then why the hell did you pay that bribe in the first place? Because that's how it works over there.
ko: 생명이 돈보다 중요하니까. 그럼 왜 처음에 뇌물을 줬어? 거기선 그렇게 돌아가니까.

en: Murder is also how it works over there. You did it. You know it. And you're not convincing anyone. Very good.
ko: 살인도 거기선 그렇게 돌아가지. 당신이 했어. 알잖아. 아무도 설득 못 해. 좋아.

en: Finally, we get to it. If you'd checked your records, you'd know the reason we won that bid is we were the only company who took it upon ourselves to go to the people.
ko: 드디어 나왔네. 기록을 확인했다면 우리가 입찰을 딴 이유가 유일하게 주민들을 찾아간 회사였기 때문이야.

en: Because you needed to see what your obstacle was. My obstacle?
ko: 장애물을 확인하려고. 내 장애물?

en: We paid $100 million more than anyone else on the condition that the money be used to relocate any family who lost anything within 50 miles of that pipeline.
ko: 파이프라인 50마일 이내에서 무엇이든 잃은 가정을 이주시키는 조건으로 다른 누구보다 1억 달러를 더 냈어.

en: Job, home, land, livestock, anything. What happened over there sickens me.
ko: 직업, 집, 땅, 가축, 무엇이든. 거기서 일어난 일이 역겹다.

en: I lose sleep over it every night. And as soon as I am out of this nightmare, I am going to shut down the entire project.
ko: 매일 밤 잠을 못 자. 이 악몽에서 벗어나면 전체 프로젝트를 중단할 거야.

en: The pipeline, the oil field, all of it. So I've just about had enough of you two wondering whether I'm guilty of these murders or not.
ko: 파이프라인, 유전, 전부. 그러니 당신 둘이 내가 이 살인에 유죄인지 의심하는 것도 이제 그만해.

en: Is that a good enough trial run for you?
ko: 리허설은 이 정도면 됐어?

en: Holy shit. She didn't do it.
ko: 세상에. 안 했어.

en: She didn't.
ko: 안 했어.
